title: Mon espace — signed-in client dashboard
problem: After magic-link sign-in, the client lands on /me — the single canonical place where their sessions, their data, and their account live. Without one canonical destination, a returning client has to remember 'where do I check my session?' across multiple URLs. /me consolidates: a tile of their sessions (with status + unread counts), a tile for starting a new intake, payment actions if a session is in pricing, language preference, optional display-name (first name), and the data-rights surface (download my data / delete my account). The page IS the client-side console; everything else (intake form, individual session views, /me/data) is reachable from it.
analysis: Auth-gated via useAuth() — a signed-out visitor is redirected to /login. Once authenticated, the page loads the session list via listSessions() (functions/api/sessions/index.ts via src/lib/sessionsApi.ts), an array of SessionRow records: id, problem_type, status, created_at, last_message_at, has_unread (computed via src/lib/unread.ts against per-session last-seen markers in localStorage). Each session renders as a card linking to /session/:id; unread sessions show a dot. Below the list: tiles for new intake (links into /intake with a fresh PendingIntake set up via PENDING_INTAKE_KEY localStorage handoff), payment actions (if any session is in pricing — PaymentActions component renders Stripe Checkout button), language preference (LangPrefCard), display name (FirstNameCard). At the bottom: data-rights — a 'download my data' link to /me/data and a destructive 'delete my account' action that calls deleteMyAccount(), sets markJustErased() (sessionStorage flag), then navigates to /au-revoir for the post-erasure ritual. Bilingual at full parity. The 'console' eyebrow framing signals this is operational, not marketing.
implementation: src/pages/MePortal.tsx — controlled by useAuth() for sign-in state; redirects to /login if not authenticated. On mount, fetches sessions via listSessions(). Tile layout uses CSS grid with named areas. Each tile is a self-contained block reading its own state. createSession() handles the new-intake flow: it primes a PendingIntake in localStorage (PENDING_INTAKE_KEY) with type + account info, then navigates to /intake which picks up the pending state and renders the appropriate problem-type form. clearDraft() purges any leftover draft on intent change. The delete-my-account action calls deleteMyAccount() (DELETE /api/me, cascades to sessions/messages/attachments via D1 ON DELETE), sets the sessionStorage flag, navigates to /au-revoir. The unread system reads a per-session last-seen timestamp from localStorage and compares against the session's last_message_at; seedIfMissing() handles first-time seeding so a newly-listed session doesn't immediately show as unread. PaymentActions reads the session list and renders Stripe Checkout only for sessions in 'pricing' status. FirstNameCard + LangPrefCard each write back via PATCH /api/me/prefs.

decisions.decision: Tile layout instead of a vertical list of sections
decisions.rationale: The dashboard has 5–6 concerns (sessions, new intake, payments, language, name, data rights). A vertical list buries data rights at the bottom; a tile layout makes each concern equally visible and accessible. Mobile collapses tiles to a single column; the visual rhythm holds.
decisions.recommendation: Keep tiles. If a seventh concern emerges, consider whether it belongs on /me at all or under a sub-route — adding tiles indefinitely turns the dashboard into a control panel.

decisions.decision: Delete-my-account hands off to /au-revoir via sessionStorage flag, not a confirmation modal
decisions.rationale: A modal-based 'are you sure?' flow followed by a server call is the dashboard-default but feels jarring for an erasure ritual. The actual confirmation lives at the API layer (DELETE /api/me requires CSRF, requires sign-in); the UX flow is: click delete → action fires → navigate to /au-revoir which carries the emotional weight of the moment. The user has time to see the post-erasure ritual instead of being dumped back on a now-empty dashboard.
decisions.recommendation: Don't add a confirm-via-typing pattern ('type DELETE to confirm') — the magic-link sign-in is itself the confirmation that this is the account owner. The /au-revoir ritual is the right amount of weight for the action.

decisions.decision: Unread tracking is per-device via localStorage, not per-account via D1
decisions.rationale: A client signing in from their phone after seeing a message on desktop shouldn't have to wade through unread-on-the-phone artifacts of what they already read. Per-device unread is the right UX even at the cost of cross-device unread state not syncing. The cost: a client clearing their browser data loses their unread state; a clean session shows everything as unread until first visit. Both are acceptable.
decisions.recommendation: Don't sync unread state via the server. If multi-device sync becomes a real complaint, consider a per-session 'last opened' timestamp on the server — but it complicates the model significantly.

successCriteria:
- A signed-in visitor lands on /me and sees their sessions, with unread indicators that match what they've read
- A signed-out visitor is redirected to /login (not shown a 401 page)
- 'New intake' navigates into /intake with the pending-intake state primed so /intake doesn't ask 'who are you?' again
- Delete-my-account triggers the full DELETE /api/me + /au-revoir ritual flow
- Language and display-name changes persist via PATCH /api/me/prefs
- Payment actions render only for sessions in pricing status
- Bilingual at full parity
userGuide: Open /me (or /en/me) — requires sign-in. The dashboard shows: your sessions (each card links to /session/:id; unread sessions show a dot), a tile for starting a new intake, payment actions if any session is in 'pricing' status, language preference, optional display name, and at the bottom a 'download my data' link (/me/data) plus a 'delete my account' button that fires the erasure flow → /au-revoir. To rename a session: open it via /session/:id; the dashboard reflects the new title on next load. To change language: the toggle in the dashboard tile (and the header) both write the same mp_lang cookie. To delete: one click; you'll see the erasure ritual at /au-revoir. There's no 'are you sure?' modal — the magic-link sign-in is the access proof.
knownLimitations: Per-device unread state isn't synced across devices — a session read on desktop shows as unread on mobile until opened there
If localStorage is cleared, all sessions appear as unread on next visit until each is opened (acceptable trade-off)

PaymentActions only renders for sessions currently in 'pricing' status — if a session is paid and then moves to building, the action disappears (which is correct), but a visitor coming back to /me later won't see history of past payments here; /session/:id is the per-session payment history
FirstNameCard / LangPrefCard each PATCH /api/me/prefs — there's no batch save; rapid edits could cause minor server load (unbatched)

The dashboard fetches the full session list at mount; for a client with many sessions, this could grow — capacity cap means the count stays bounded, but a future infinite-session model would need pagination

statusHistory.reason: Page shipped with tile layout, session list, intake handoff, and delete-my-account flow.
Added markJustErased() handoff so the delete-my-account action navigates to /au-revoir with the ritual flag set; integrates with the ton-passage arc.
Code-first doc co-located. Erasure flow now end-to-end with the /au-revoir ritual; behaviour stable.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Initial author — P2 auth-gated sweep, co-located doc for /me. Captures the recent markJustErased() handoff added in f3ef8f1.
